# sourcevision

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 146
- **Lines of code**: 44,803
- **Languages**: TypeScript (134), JSON (6), Markdown (3), Other (3)
- **Zones**: 9 (9 with descriptions)
- **Git**: main @ 63d150a
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-10T00:48:16.636Z

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- God function: cmdAnalyze in src/cli/commands/analyze.ts calls 32 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Observations

- Isolated files — no import edges between 2 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0) [info] (.claude)
- 19 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (analyzers)
- Generic zone name "Analyzers 2" — enrichment did not assign a meaningful name reflecting this zone's domain purpose [warning] (analyzers-2)
- High coupling (0.52) — 15 imports target "analyzers" [warning] (analyzers-2)
- Generic zone name "Analyzers 3" — enrichment did not assign a meaningful name reflecting this zone's domain purpose [warning] (analyzers-3)
- High coupling (0.73) — 13 imports target "analyzers" [warning] (analyzers-3)
- Low cohesion (0.27) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (analyzers-3)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (e2e)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (fixtures)
- Bidirectional coupling: "analyzers" ↔ "unit" (5+29 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- ... and 5 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "analyzers" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (analyzers)
- Zone "cli" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (cli)
- Zone "fixtures" has files across 5 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (fixtures)
- src/analyzers/ contains files from 5 zones (analyzers, analyzers-2, analyzers-3, cli, unit) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Hub function: info in src/cli/output.ts is called from 9 files (high fan-in expected for this module type) — changes here have wide impact, consider if responsibilities can be narrowed [info]
- Zone "unit" has files across 10 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (unit)
- Merge analyzers-2 into the main analyzers zone. The 11 files (callgraph.ts, components.ts, imports.ts, inventory.ts, route-detection.ts, index.ts, paths.ts, and 4 unit tests) all reside in src/analyzers/ and tests/unit/analyzers/ and are logically part of the same analysis engine. The zone boundary is a Louvain artifact caused by zones.ts acting as a hub — the algorithm clustered hub-adjacent files into a satellite zone. After merge: move src/util/paths.ts to src/util/ (it already lives there; remove it from this zone membership) and verify src/analyzers/index.ts is the sole public entry point for the merged zone. [warning] (analyzers-2)
- Rename 'analyzers-2' to a semantically meaningful name by inspecting its file contents and choosing a name following the '{domain}-{role}' convention (finding 8). Numeric-suffix auto-generated names make this zone unreferenceable in architecture discussions and prevent meaningful cohesion/coupling comparisons across passes. [warning] (analyzers-2)
- Merge analyzers-3 into the main analyzers zone rather than renaming or splitting it. The three production files (zones.ts, louvain.ts, zone-hash.ts) are the zone-detection subsystem of the same analysis engine. After merge, the zones.ts decomposition task becomes tractable: the three test files (zone-detection.test.ts, zone-size-policy.test.ts, zone-subdivision.test.ts) already map to three internal sub-concerns (detection, size constraints, subdivision) that can guide extraction of focused modules from zones.ts when the god-function metric warrants it (currently 32 calls, 2x above any reasonable threshold). Do not decompose zones.ts before the zone-public.ts re-export boundary is in place. [warning] (analyzers-3)
- Zone "Analyzers 3" (analyzers-3) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.73, cohesion: 0.27, coupling: 0.73) — requires immediate architectural intervention [critical] (analyzers-3)
- ... and 2 more

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
